model_input:
You are a DVOC (Draft-Verify-Action Loop) verifier. A red crosshair shows the current predicted click position on the screen. A fixed blue circle provides spatial reference.

Call report_offset(dx, dy, confidence) with the pixel offset from the crosshair to the target element center.
dx: pixels right (negative = left). dy: pixels down (negative = up).
confidence: 0.0 (uncertain) to 1.0 (certain).

Only report dx=0, dy=0 when the crosshair is within a few pixels of the target. If uncertain, set lower confidence so the loop keeps refining.

Task: Click the Docs link in the navigation
Previous iterations:
  iter 0: at (184,426) said dx=151 dy=-550 conf=0.90 -> (335,-124)
  iter 1: at (335,0) said dx=35 dy=44 conf=0.80 -> (370,44)
Call report_offset().

raw_message.reasoning_content:
The user wants to click the "Docs" link in the navigation bar.
The current crosshair is at a position indicated by the red crosshair inside a blue circle.
Looking at the image, the red crosshair is centered around the blue circle.
The "Docs" link is to the right of the crosshair.
Let's estimate the center of the "Docs" link.
The crosshair is at (approximately) x=270, y=45 based on visual inspection.
The "Docs" link text is roughly from x=275 to x=305.
Wait, let's look at the previous iterations.
Iter 1: at (335, 0) said dx=35 dy=44 -> moved to (370, 44). This seems wrong, it moved down and right.
Let's re-examine the current image.
The blue circle is roughly at x=270, y=45.
The text "Docs" is centered at roughly x=290, y=45.
Let's refine this.
The nav bar is at the top.
"Pricing" is to the left. "Docs" is to the right.
The blue circle is between "Pricing" and "Docs".
The center of the "Docs" link appears to be slightly to the right of the crosshair.
The red crosshair center seems to be at x=270, y=45.
The "Docs" text starts at x=276 and ends at x=305.
Center of "Docs" is around x=290.
So dx = 290 - 270 = 20.
dy = 45 - 45 = 0.
Let's re-read the crosshair position.
The blue circle/crosshair is between "Pricing" and "Docs".
Actually, looking closely at the crosshair, it's centered on a vertical line that separates Pricing and Docs (or rather, it's on the text "Docs").
Wait, the text "Docs" is partially covered by the blue circle.
The blue circle is around x=270.
The "Docs" text is centered at approximately x=290.
Let's try dx=20, dy=0.
Looking at the image coordinates:
The image is 1000x500 (normalized? no, standard pixel coordinates).
Wait, the small text at bottom right says (352, 22) +/- 30px. This might be the blue circle position if it were a tool, but I should use the visual.
The red crosshair is at roughly x=270, y=45.
The "Docs" text center is around x=290, y=45.
So dx=20, dy=0.
Confidence: 0.8.
